hadiths.chapter: 147. CHAPTER ON THE REASON FOR NEGLECTING HAJJ
hadiths.en:
Abu Hamzah al-Thumali narrated from Abu Ja'far {a.s}, who said:  
"I heard him say: 'No servant gives preference to a worldly need over performing Hajj 
except that he will see the pilgrims, having completed their rituals and shaved their 
heads, return before his need is fulfilled.'"   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 147, H. 2863

hadiths.chapter: 148. CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
hadiths.ar:
2865   -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّن ْذُّ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ُّ سِن َّان ٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال 
كَّن ِي زإً لَّمْ ي َّحُّح  َّ ق َّظ ُّ وَّ لَّمْ ي ُّطِق ِ إَّلْحَّح  َّ لِكِن َّزِةِ إ َّن ْ ي ُّح َّهِّز َّ رَّج ُّلاً ي َّحُّح  ُّ عَّن ْه
hadiths.en:
Al-Halabi narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who said:  
"If a person is financially capable but is prevented from performing Hajj due to illness 
or another matter for which Allah {SWT}, the Exalted, excuses him, then it is incumbent 
upon him to have Hajj performed on his behalf from his wealth by someone who has 
not performed Hajj before and does not have wealth of their own."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 63  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 148, H. 2864

hadiths.chapter: 148. CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
hadiths.ar:
2866  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ مُّعَّاوِي َّه ُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّم َّارٍ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ جَّح  َّ عَّن ْ ع َّي ْزِةِ إ َّ ي ُّح ْز ِي هِ ذ َّلِك َّ عَّن ْ جَّح  َّه ِ 
إَّلْأ ِسْلاَّمِ ق َّال
hadiths.en:
Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who said:  
"Indeed, the Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali Ibn Abi Talib {a.s} instructed an elderly 
man who had never performed Hajj and was unable to do so due to his old age to 
arrange for someone to perform Hajj on his behalf."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 273 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 275 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 65  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 148, H. 2865

hadiths.chapter: 148. CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
hadiths.ar:
2867   -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّلِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  جَّمْز َّة َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ن َّصِي زٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال 
رَّج ُّل  كَّان َّث ْ لَّهُّ جَّح  َّه   ق َّا ِن ْ إ َّي ْسَّزَّ ن َّعْذَّ ذ َّلِك َّ كَّان َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلْحَّح  ُّ وَّ كَّذ َّلِك َّ إَّلن  َّاصِث ُّ إ ِذ َّإ عَّزَّف َّ ق َّعَّلَّ ي ْهِ إَّلْحَّح  ُّ وَّ إ ِ ن ْ كَّان َّ ق َّذْ جَّح  َّ.
hadiths.en:
Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about a man who performed 
Hajj on behalf of someone else: "Does it suffice for him as his Hajj of Islam?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "Yes."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 148, H. 2866

hadiths.chapter: 148. CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
hadiths.ar:
2868 - وَّ رَّوَّى سَّعْذُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّن ْ مُّوسَّى ن ْن ِ إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّلِى ٍّ إ َّجْمَّذَّ ن ْن ِ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ مُّطَّه َّزٍ ق َّالَّ : كَّي َّن ْث ُّ إ ِلَّى  
إ َّن ِى  مُّحَّم َّذٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ ِن ِّى  ذَّق َّعْث ُّ إ ِلَّى سِي  َّه ِ إ َّن ْق ُّسٍ مِان َّه َّ ذِي ن َّارٍ وَّ ج َّمْسِي ن َّ ذِي ن َّارإً لِي َّحُّح  ُّ وإ ن ِهَّا ق َّزَّج َّعُّوإ وَّ لَّمْ ي َّس ْح َّصْ  
ن َّعْص ُّهُّمْ وَّ إ َّي َّان ِى  ن َّعْص   ق َّذ َّكَّزَّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّذْ إ َّن ْق َّق َّ  ن َّعْص َّ إَّلذ َّن َّان ِي زِ وَّ ن َّق ِي َّث ْ ن َّق ِي  َّه   وَّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ي َّزُّذ ُّ عَّلَّى  َّ مَّا ن َّق ِى َّ وَّ إ ِن ِّى  ق َّذْ رُّمْث ُّ مُّطَّالَّن َّه َّ 
مَّن ْ لَّمْ ي َّا ْي ِن ِى  ن ِمَّا ذَّق َّعْث ُّ إ ِلَّي ْهِ ق َّكَّي َّث َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم  مِم َّن ْ إ َّي َّاك َّ  س َّي ْن اً مِم َّا ي َّا ْي ِي ك َّ ن ِهِ 
وَّ إَّلْأ َّج ْزُّ ق َّذْ وَّق َّغَّ عَّلَّى إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل
hadiths.en:
Ali ibn Abi Hamzah narrated from Abu Basir, from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who said:  
"If an impoverished man is sent for Hajj by another person, it will count as a Hajj for 
him. However, if he later becomes financially capable, Hajj will still be obligatory upon 
him. Similarly, a Nasibi (one who opposes the Ahlulbayt), if he comes to recognize the 
truth, Hajj becomes obligatory upon him, even if he had already performed it 
previously."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 273 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 9 • 
Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 293  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 148, H. 2867

hadiths.chapter: 148. CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
hadiths.en:
Sa'd ibn Abdullah narrated from Musa ibn al -Hasan, from Abu Ali Ahmad ibn 
Muhammad ibn Mutahhar, who said:   
“I wrote to Abu Muhammad {a.s}: "I gave 150 dinars to six people so they could perform 
Hajj. Some of them returned without departing for Hajj, and some came to me, stating 
that they had spent part of the money and still had some remaining, which they 
intended to return to me. I have co nsidered demanding what I gave to those who did 
not return to me."  
Imam {a.s} wrote back: "Do not pursue those who did not return to you, and do not take 
anything from those who came back with what they had. The reward is already with 
Allah {SWT}, the Exalted."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 322 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 180  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 148, H. 2868
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٤٨، ح ٢٨٦٨

hadiths.chapter: 148. CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
hadiths.ar:
2870  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ج َّمِي لُّ ن ْن ُّ ذَّر َّإح ٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ لَّي ْسَّ لَّهُّ مَّال  جَّح  َّ عَّن ْ رَّج لٍ إ َّوْ إ َّجَّح  َّهُّ 
ع َّي ْزُّةُّ ي ُّم َّ إ َّصَّاب َّ مَّالأً هَّلْ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلْحَّح  ُّ ق َّق َّال
hadiths.en:
Al-Bazanti narrated from Abu al-Hasan {a.s}, who said:  
I asked him about a man who received funds for Hajj from someone, but his path was 
blocked by bandits. Then another person provided him with funds for Hajj. Is it 
permissible for him to accept this?  
Imam {a.s} replied: "It is permissible for him to do so. The reward will be accounted for 
both the first and the second provider, and he had no other option but to do what he 
did when he found someone willing to provide for his Hajj."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 323 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 191  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 148, H. 2869
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO

hadiths.chapter: 148. CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
hadiths.ar:
2871   -  وَّ ق ِي لَّ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : إَّلز َّج ُّلُّ ي َّا ْج ُّذ ُّ إَّلْحَّح  َّه َّ مِن َّ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ق َّي َّمُّوب ُّ ق َّلاَّ ي َّي ْز ك ُّ س َّي ْن اً ق َّق َّال 
عَّن ِ إَّلْمَّي ِّث ِ وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّث ْ لَّهُّ عِن ْذَّ إَّلل َّهِ جَّح  َّه   إ ُّي ْن ِي َّث ْ لِصَّاجِن ِهِ
hadiths.en:
Jamil ibn Darraj narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s} regarding a man who did not 
have wealth and performed Hajj on behalf of another person or was sent for Hajj by 
someone else. Then, later, he acquired wealth. Is Hajj still obligatory upon him?  
Imam {a.s} said: "It suffices for both."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 296 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 57  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 148, H. 2870
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٤٨، ح ٢٨٧٠

hadiths.chapter: 148. CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
hadiths.ar:
2872   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ سَّعِي ذُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ إَّلْأ َّعْزَّح ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ِ إَّلص َّزُّورَّة ِ إ َّ ي َّحُّح عَّن ِ إَّلْمَّي ِّث ِ ق َّق َّال 
إ ِذ َّإ لَّمْ ي َّح ِذِ إَّلص َّزُّورَّة ُّ مَّا ي َّحُّح  ُّ ن ِهِ وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ لَّهُّ مَّال  ق َّلَّي ْسَّ لَّهُّ ذ َّلِك َّ جَّي  َّى ي َّحُّح  َّ مِن ْ مَّالِهِ وَّ هُّوَّ ي ُّح ْز ِى  عَّن ِ إَّلْمَّي ِّث ِ كَّان َّ  
لَّهُّ مَّال  إ َّوْ لَّمْ ي َّكُّن ْ لَّهُّ مَّال
hadiths.en:
It was said to Abu Abdullah {a.s}: "A man takes funds for Hajj from someone, 
but he dies and leaves nothing behind."  
Imam {a.s} said: "It suffices for the deceased, and if he had a Hajj recorded with Allah 
{SWT}, it will be credited to the one who provided the funds."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 148, H. 2871
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٤٨، ح ٢٨٧١

hadiths.chapter: 148. CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
hadiths.ar:
2873  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحَّسَّن ُّ ن ْن ُّ مَّحْن ُّوب ٍ عَّن ْ عَّلِى ِّ ن ْن ِ رِن َّاب ٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ إ َّعْ طَّى رَّج ُّلاً جَّح  َّه ً 
ي َّحُّح  ُّ ن ِهَّا عَّن ْهُّ مِن َّ إَّلْكُّوق َّه ِ ق َّحَّح  َّ ن ِهَّا عَّن ْهُّ مِن َّ إَّلْن َّصْزَّة ِ ق َّال  ق َّق َّذْ ي َّم َّ جَّح  ُّه
hadiths.en:
Sa'id ibn Abdullah al -A'raj asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about a person who has 
not performed Hajj (a sarura): "Can they perform Hajj on behalf of a deceased 
person?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes, if the sarura does not have the means to perform Hajj for 
themselves. However, if they have wealth, they cannot do so until they perform Hajj 
for themselves from their own wealth. Performing Hajj on behalf of the deceased is 
valid whether the deceased had wealth or not."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 312  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 148, H. 2872
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٤٨، ح ٢٨٧٢

hadiths.chapter: 148. CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
hadiths.ar:
2874  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إِن ْن ُّ مَّحْن ُّوب ٍ عَّن ْ هِس َّامِ ن ْن ِ سَّالِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ن َّصِي زٍ عَّن ْ إ َّجَّذِهِمَّا عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ إ َّعْطَّى رَّج ُّلاً 
ذَّرَّإهِمَّ ي َّحُّح  ُّ ن ِهَّا عَّن ْهُّ جَّح  َّه ً مُّق ْزَّذَّة ً إ َّ ي َّح ُّور ُّ لَّهُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّي َّمَّي  َّغ مْ إ ِن  َّمَّا ج َّالَّق َّهُّ إ ِلَّى إَّلْق َّص ْلِ وَّ 
إَّلْح َّي ْزِ
hadiths.en:
Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Ali ibn Ri'ab, from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, 
regarding a man who gave funds to another person to perform Hajj on his behalf from 
Kufa, but the person performed Hajj on his behalf from Basra.  
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm in it. If he completed all the rites of Hajj, then the Hajj 
is valid."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 3, p. 154  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 148, H. 2873
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٤٨، ح ٢٨٧٣

hadiths.chapter: 148. CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
hadiths.ar:
2875 - وَّ ق َّالَّ وَّهْث ُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّن ْذِ رَّن ِّهِ لِلص َّاذِق ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : إ َّ ي َّحُّح  ُّ إَّلز َّج ُّلُّ عَّن ِ إَّلن  َّاصِث ِ ق َّق َّال لْث ُّ ق َّا ِن ْ كَّان َّ إ َّن ِى  
ق َّق َّال
hadiths.en:
Ibn Mahbub narrated from Hisham ibn Salim, from Abu Basir, from one of the 
Imams {a.s}, regarding a man who gave another person money to perform a single Hajj 
(Hajjah Mufradah) on his behalf.  
The question was asked: "Is it permissible for him to perform the Tamattu' (combining 
'Umrah and Hajj) instead?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes, he has only deviated to what is more virtuous and better."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 307 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 415 
• Al-Istibsar, Part 2, p. 323 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 317 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 182  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 148, H. 2874

hadiths.chapter: 148. CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
hadiths.ar:
2876  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ : إ َّن  َّ إَّلص َّاذِق َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّعْطَّى رَّج ُّلاً ي َّلاَّي ِي ن َّ ذِي ن َّارإً ق َّق َّالَّ لَّه   وَّ إِق ْعَّلْ وَّ إِق ْعَّلْ  
وَّ لَّك َّ ي ِسْغ  وَّ لَّهُّ وَّإجِذَّة
hadiths.en:
Wahb ibn Abd Rabbih said to Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s}: "Can 
a man perform Hajj on behalf of a Nasibi (one who harbors enmity toward the 
Ahlulbayt)?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "No."  
I said: "What if it was my father?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "If it was your father, then perform Hajj on his behalf."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 148, H. 2875

hadiths.chapter: 148. CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
hadiths.ar:
2877  -  وَّرَّوَّى إ َّن َّان ُّ ن ْن ُّ عُّي ْمَّان َّ، عَّن ْ ي َّحْي َّى إلْأ َّر ْرَّق ِ، عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إلس َّلَّامُّ، ق َّالَّ: "مَّن ْ جَّح  َّ عَّن ْ إ ِن ْسَّان ٍ إ س ْي َّزَّكَّا  
جَّي  َّى إ ِذ َّإ ق َّص َّى طَّوَّإف َّ إلْق َّزِي ص َّه ِ إ ن ْق َّطَّعَّث ِ إ لس  َّزِكَّه ُّ، ق َّمَّا كَّان َّ ن َّعْذَّ ذ َّلِك َّ مِن ْ عَّمَّلٍ كَّان َّ لِذ َّلِك َّ إ لْحَّاح ِّ ."
hadiths.en:
It was narrated that Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} gave a man thirty 
dinars and said to him:  
"Perform Hajj on behalf of Isma'il, and do this and that, and you shall have nine 
[portions of reward], while he shall have one."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 339 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 165  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 148, H. 2876

hadiths.chapter: 148. CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
hadiths.ar:
2878  -  وَّ ق َّالَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ إ َّعْطَّى رَّج ُّلاً مَّالأً ي َّحُّح  ُّ عَّن ْهُّ ق َّحَّح  َّ عَّن ْ ن َّق ْسِهِ ق َّق َّال جِث ِ إَّلْمَّالِ    
ولأ ن ا س إ ن  ي حح  إلمزإ ة  عن  إلمزإ ة ، وإلمزإ ة  عن  إلزج ل ، وإلزج ل عن  إلمزإ ة  وإلزج ل عن  إلزج ل.   
ولأ ن ا س إ ن  ي حح  إلصزورة  عن  إلصزورة  ، وإلصزورة  عن  ع ي ز إلصزورة ، وع ي ز إلصزورة  عن  إلصزورة .
hadiths.en:
Aban ibn Uthman narrated from Yahya al -Azraq, from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who 
said:  
"Whoever performs Hajj on behalf of another person, they share in the reward until the 
obligatory Tawaf is completed. After that, any act performed is for the one performing 
the Hajj."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 148, H. 2877

hadiths.chapter: 148. CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
hadiths.ar:
2879   -  وَّ رَّوَّى جَّزِي ز   عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ مُّسْلِمٍ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ِ إَّلص َّزُّورَّة ِ إ َّ ي َّحُّح  ُّ مِن ْ مَّالِ  
إَّلز  َّكَّاة ِ ق َّال
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said regarding a man who gave money to another to perform Hajj on 
his behalf, but the person performed Hajj for himself instead:   
"It is on behalf of the one who provided the money."   
[AL SADUQ] 
There is no harm if a woman performs Hajj on behalf of a woman, a woman on behalf 
of a man, a man on behalf of a woman, or a man on behalf of a man.   
Similarly, there is no harm if a person who has not performed Hajj ( sarura) performs 
Hajj on behalf of another sarura, a sarura on behalf of someone who has performed 
Hajj, or someone who has performed Hajj on behalf of a sarura.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 2 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 461 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 
321 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 193  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 148, H. 2878

hadiths.chapter: 148. CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
hadiths.ar:
2880  - وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ مُّعَّاوِي َّه َّ ن ْن ِ عَّم َّارٍ ق َّالَّ : ق ُّلْث ُّ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إَّلز َّج ُّلُّ ي َّح ْزُّح ُّ ق ِى ي ِح َّارَّة ٍ إ ِلَّى مَّك َّه َّ إ َّوْ 
ي َّكُّون ُّ لَّهُّ إ ِن ِل  ق َّي ُّكْزِي هَّا جَّح  َّي ُّهُّ ن َّاق ِصَّه   إ َّوْ ي َّام َّه   ق َّال ام َّه  .
hadiths.en:
Hariz narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, who said:  
I asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about a sarura (one who has not performed Hajj): "Can they 
perform Hajj using Zakat funds?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "Yes."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 2 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 460 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 10, p. 
177 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 9, p. 290 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 207  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 148, H. 2879

hadiths.chapter: 148. CHAPTER ON DELEGATING HAJJ TO SOMEONE WHO
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar, who said:  
I said to Abu Abdullah {a.s}: "A man travels for trade to Mecca or has camels that he 
rents out. Is his Hajj considered incomplete or complete?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "No, rather his Hajj is complete."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 275 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 299 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 59

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 148, H. 2880

hadiths.chapter: 149. CHAPTER ON THE HAJJ OF THE CAMEL DRIVER AND
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar, who said:  
I said to Abu Abdullah {a.s}: "Is the Hajj of a camel driver complete or incomplete?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "It is complete."  
I asked: "Is the Hajj of a hired worker complete or incomplete?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "It is complete."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 58

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 149, H. 2881

hadiths.chapter: 150. CHAPTER ON ONE WHO DIES WHILE OWING THE
hadiths.en:
Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Ali ibn Ri'ab, from Durays al-Kunasi, who 
said:  
I asked Abu Ja'far {a.s} about a man who was obligated to perform the Hajj of Islam but 
made a vow of gratitude to send another man to perform Hajj to Mecca on his behalf. 
The man died before performing the Hajj of Islam and before fulfilling the vow he had 
made.  
Imam {a.s} said: "If he left wealth, the Hajj of Islam should be performed on his behalf 
from his entire estate, and from one -third of his estate, funds should be allocated to 
send a man to fulfill the vow he made. In this way, the vow will have been fulfilled. 
However, if he left only enough wealth for the Hajj of Islam, it should be used for that 
purpose, and his heir should perform the Hajj of the vow on his behalf, as the vow is 
akin to a debt upon him."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 11, p. 528 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 11, p. 74

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 150, H. 2882
